nodes.metadata_json:
{"branch":"master","confidence":95,"files":["core/src/protocol","core/test/protocol","core/src/index.ts"],"prompt":"Repository: /Users/qdot/code/qrtuber

Implement Stage A1 from plans/fill-in-the-rest-implementation.md in the qrtuber repo.

Ownership/write scope: core/src/protocol/**, core/test/protocol/**, and core/src/index.ts only. You are not alone in the codebase; do not revert or overwrite edits outside your scope, and accommodate concurrent changes if present.

Use TDD-style vertical slices. Implement the public protocol API exactly as planned:
- core/src/protocol/: charset.ts with QR_ALPHANUMERIC_REGEX /^[0-9A-Z $%*+\-./:]*$/; HapticsState.ts; frames.ts with PROTOCOL_VERSION "QT1", MAX_SEQ 4294967295, MAX_FRAME_LENGTH 256, SESSION_REGEX, HapticsFrame, FrameParseError union EMPTY|TOO_LONG|NOT_ALPHANUMERIC|BAD_STRUCTURE|BAD_VERSION|BAD_SESSION|BAD_SEQ|UNKNOWN_TYPE|BAD_PAYLOAD|BAD_EXTENSION; parseFrame.ts; encodeFrame.ts; SequenceTracker.ts; index.ts.
- parseFrame(text): QRTuberFrame | null, never throws, null on malformed input.
- parseFrameResult(text): discriminated union with error reason, validation order matching the error list.
- encodeFrame(frame): string, throws TypeError/RangeError for programmer input.
- HapticsState wraps 9 bytes; fromHex strict 18 uppercase hex or null; toHex/get/toArray/equals/isAllZero; constructor clamps 0-255 and rounds (NaN -> 0, 300 -> 255, 12.6 -> 13).
- SequenceTracker accept(frame): first true; session change true; same session+same seq false; any different seq true; reset.
- Grammar: QT1:<SESSION>:<SEQ>:<TYPE>:<PAYLOAD>[:K:<V>...] with session /^[0-9A-Z]{1,8}$/, seq u32 decimal, H payload 18 hex chars, extension tail single-uppercase-letter keys with non-empty values; unknown keys ignored but malformed tail rejects.
- Use .js extensions on intra-core imports.
- Barrel export from core/src/index.ts.

Tests: create core/test/protocol/*.test.ts covering the matrix in the plan: spec vector QT1:A7F2:18422:H:00FF4080A010203040; clamping; strict fromHex; seq edge cases; session length/charset; extension tail well-formed/odd/two-char-key/empty-value; garbage never throws; encode/parse roundtrip; encode throws on bad session/seq; SequenceTracker accept/dup/restart/session-change/reset.

Run `npm test -w qrtuber` and `npm run compile -w qrtuber`. Do not commit. Final response: summarize changed files and verification results."}

nodes.metadata_json:
{"branch":"master","confidence":95,"prompt":"Repository: /Users/qdot/code/qrtuber

Implement Stage A2 from plans/fill-in-the-rest-implementation.md.

Ownership/write scope: core/src/adapters/**, core/test/adapters/**, core/package.json, package-lock.json, core/src/index.ts, core/src/HttpForwarder.ts, core/src/IntifaceClient.ts. You are not alone in the codebase; do not revert or overwrite edits outside your scope, and accommodate concurrent changes if present. Do not commit.

Task:
- Bump core dependency `buttplug` to `^5.0.1` and update the root lockfile with npm as needed.
- Add `core/src/adapters/IntifaceHapticsAdapter.ts` targeting Buttplug JS 5.x with a locally-defined injectable client/device/feature interface.
- Delete legacy `core/src/HttpForwarder.ts` and `core/src/IntifaceClient.ts`, and update `core/src/index.ts` barrel accordingly.

Adapter contract:
- Constructor `(options?: IntifaceHapticsAdapterOptions, client?: ButtplugClientLike)`.
- Defaults: address `ws://127.0.0.1:12345`, clientName `QRTuber`, sequential channel map where global actuator ordinal maps `% 9`, frameTimeoutMs 2000.
- Methods: `connect()`, `disconnect()`, `applyState(state: HapticsState)`, `stopAll()`, getter `connected`.
- Use Buttplug 5 API names verified against installed/packed types: `ButtplugClient`, `ButtplugBrowserWebsocketClientConnector`, `DeviceOutput`, `OutputType`, client `devices: ReadonlyMap<number, device>`, device `features: ReadonlyMap<number, feature>`, feature `outputs`, `runOutput(DeviceOutput.Vibrate.percent(value))`, device/client stop methods as appropriate.
- Deduplicate identical consecutive states, but resend if device/feature count changes.
- Map byte 0-255 to percent 0-1. Clamp custom mapped channel indexes to 0..8. Sequential ordinal mapping wraps the 10th actuator to channel 0.
- Skip zero-actuator devices. Per-device/feature try/catch so one failure does not block others.
- Watchdog: frameTimeoutMs arms/rearms on applyState; firing calls stopAll and clears cache; 0 disables; disconnect clears watchdog. stopAll zeroes all vibrate features and clears cached state.
- Not connected applyState is a no-op.

Tests:
- Add `core/test/adapters/IntifaceHapticsAdapter.test.ts` with fake client/devices/features and `vi.useFakeTimers()`.
- Cover defaults/address override; connect passes websocket connector; multi-device ordinal mapping (1 + 2 actuators -> ch0 / ch1,ch2; 10th wraps ch0); custom + out-of-range channelMap clamped; byte->percent conversion; not-connected no-op; zero-actuator device skipped; dedupe identical twice -> one call; device-count change re-sends; watchdog arm/re-arm/fire -> stopAll -> cache cleared; timeout 0 disables; rejecting device/feature doesn't block others; disconnect clears watchdog.

Run `npm test -w qrtuber`, `npm run compile -w qrtuber`, and `grep -rn "HttpForwarder\|QRTuberIntifaceClient" core/src extension docs/src webrtc/src` (expected no hits after deletion/update, aside from unrelated built dist if present should be ignored). Final response: summarize changed files and verification results."}

{"branch":"master","confidence":95,"files":["core/src/visual","core/test/visual","core/src/QRCodeFinder.ts","core/src/ContentVideoHandler.ts","core/src/index.ts","extension/entrypoints/background.ts","extension/entrypoints/content.ts","docs/src/components/DemoPage/DemoReactComponent.tsx"],"prompt":"Repository: /Users/qdot/code/qrtuber

Implement Stage A3 from plans/fill-in-the-rest-implementation.md. This is the raw-payload cutover across core and current consumers. You are not alone in the codebase; do not revert unrelated edits, and accommodate existing A0-A2 commits. Do not commit.

Ownership/write scope:
- core/src/visual/**, core/test/visual/**
- core/src/QRCodeFinder.ts
- core/src/ContentVideoHandler.ts
- core/src/index.ts
- extension/entrypoints/background.ts
- extension/entrypoints/content.ts
- docs/src/components/DemoPage/DemoReactComponent.tsx

Core tasks:
- Add `core/src/visual/types.ts` with `Point`, `BoundingBox`, `VisualDecodeResult`, and `boundingBoxFromPoints(points)` returning null for empty arrays.
- Add `core/test/visual/boundingBox.test.ts` covering empty->null, single point, 4-corner, unordered/negative.
- Refactor `QRCodeFinder.ts` per contract: remove `QRCodeFinderResult`, remove inline speed parse/TODO; keep the zbar import and existing barbarian comment byte-for-byte. `findQRCode(source?: Blob | ImageData): Promise<VisualDecodeResult | null>` returns raw payload + bbox; if no source and no staged blob return null instead of crashing. Keep `getBlobFromURL` for interim consumers. `DETECTION_EVENT` emits `VisualDecodeResult`.
- Refactor `ContentVideoHandler.ts` per contract: import visual types; `startTrackingVideo(video?: HTMLVideoElement)` explicit element or fallback `querySelector('video')`; `stopTrackingVideo()` fully resets: abort listeners, revoke blob URL, clear ROI state; getter `currentRegion(): BoundingBox | null`; `handleQRCodeFinderReturn(result: VisualDecodeResult | null)` guards `videoElem===null` so pending decode after stop dies; use AbortController for all video listeners; delete broken debugLog, trackingInterval, dead/commented code. Preserve +10px ROI border and ROI state.
- Update `core/src/index.ts` final barrel: ContentVideoHandler, QRCodeFinder, protocol, adapter, visual/types.

Consumer tasks:
- `extension/entrypoints/background.ts`: bare `qrtuber` imports; QR decode response/DETECTION_EVENT -> parseFrame -> SequenceTracker.accept -> IntifaceHapticsAdapter.applyState. Drop dead attach/detach cases. Keep blob_url + connect/disconnect routing for now; Stage C rewrites it. Background decode path must catch errors and send a response instead of hanging.
- `extension/entrypoints/content.ts`: replace deep import with bare `qrtuber`; delete commented header. Ensure sendMessage errors do not create unbounded failures.
- `docs/src/components/DemoPage/DemoReactComponent.tsx`: use QRCodeFinder, SequenceTracker, IntifaceHapticsAdapter. Display raw payload as trackedValue. Pipeline parseFrame -> accept -> applyState. Do not keep legacy speed bridge.

Verification:
- `npm test -w qrtuber`
- `npm run compile -w qrtuber`
- `npm run compile -w qrtuber-extension`
- `npm run typecheck -w qrtuber-docs`
- `npm run build -w qrtuber-docs` if reasonably possible
- `grep -rn "QRTuberIntifaceClient\|QRCodeFinderResult\|intiface_command: \"speed\"" extension docs/src webrtc/src core/src` should have no hits.

Final response: list changed files and verification results."}

{"branch":"master","confidence":95,"prompt":"Repository: /Users/qdot/code/qrtuber

Implement Stage C1 from plans/fill-in-the-rest-implementation.md. Do not commit.

Ownership/write scope: extension/utils/messages.ts, extension/utils/status.ts, extension/utils/settings.ts, extension/utils/engine-client.ts only. You are not alone in the codebase; do not revert or overwrite edits outside your scope.

Requirements:
- `messages.ts`: define typed message protocol and runtime validators/type guards for externally received messages.
  - `EngineRequest` union: `frame/decode {seq, dataUrl, mode: 'search'|'roi', roiOrigin}`, `tracking/started`, `tracking/stopped {reason: 'user'|'no-video'|'navigation'}`, `intiface/connect`, `intiface/disconnect`, `intiface/devices/get`, `haptics/set-enabled`, `status/get`.
  - `EnsureEngineRequest {type:'engine/ensure'}`.
  - `FrameDecodeResponse {found, boundingBox|error}`.
  - `DeviceInfo/DeviceActuator`.
  - `ContentCommand` ping/start/stop.
  - `StatusBroadcast` for status/update.
  - Export validators: `isEngineRequest`, `isEnsureEngineRequest`, `isContentCommand`, `isStatusBroadcast`, plus any smaller validators useful for handlers.
  - Use VisualDecodeResult/BoundingBox types from `qrtuber` when helpful.
- `status.ts`: `QRTuberStatus { intiface: {state, deviceCount, address, error}, tracking: {state: idle|searching|tracking|no-video, tabId, lastDecode: {session, seq, at}|null}, hapticsEnabled, lastError }` and a `createInitialStatus(address?: string)` helper if useful.
- `settings.ts`: WXT storage items from `wxt/utils/storage`: intifaceAddress fallback `ws://127.0.0.1:12345`, autoConnect true, mappingMode `'simple'|'mapped'`, channelMap `(ChannelMapping|null)[9]` where `ChannelMapping = {deviceName, actuatorIndex, scale}`, decodeStaleTimeoutMs 1500. Include a helper to build the default 9-null channel map if useful.
- `engine-client.ts`: `ensureEngine()`, `sendToEngine<T>()` (ensure-then-send, one retry), `broadcastSafe()` fire-and-forget wrapper.

Style:
- TypeScript strict, no any where avoidable.
- Runtime validators should reject malformed external messages without throwing.
- Use .js extension in relative imports if importing local modules.

Verification:
- `npm run compile -w qrtuber-extension`
- `npm run build -w qrtuber-extension`
- `npm run build:firefox -w qrtuber-extension`

Final response: list changed files and verification results."}

nodes.metadata_json:
{"branch":"master","confidence":95,"prompt":"Repository: /Users/qdot/code/qrtuber

Implement Stage C2 from plans/fill-in-the-rest-implementation.md. Do not commit.

Ownership/write scope:
- extension/lib/engine/**
- extension/entrypoints/offscreen/**
- extension/entrypoints/background.ts
You are not alone in the codebase; do not revert unrelated edits and do not edit popup/content/options yet.

Context:
- C1 utilities exist under extension/utils: messages.ts, status.ts, settings.ts, engine-client.ts.
- Core exports `QRCodeFinder`, `parseFrame`, `SequenceTracker`, `IntifaceHapticsAdapter`, `HapticsState`, visual types.
- Current background.ts is interim from Stage A3 and should be rewritten.

Requirements:
- Add `extension/lib/engine/Engine.ts` importing core only from `qrtuber` plus C1 utilities/settings.
  - Owns a `QRTuberStatus` object.
  - Handles validated `EngineRequest`s only; reject malformed messages without throwing.
  - Decode path: fetch(dataUrl) -> blob -> QRCodeFinder.findQRCode(blob) -> parseFrame -> SequenceTracker.accept -> decode gate -> channel mapping -> adapter.applyState.
  - hapticsEnabled false should suppress device output and call/keep stopAll as appropriate.
  - staleness timer using `decodeStaleTimeoutMs` setting; on stale, zero devices/stopAll and update status.
  - watch `intifaceAddress`; reconnect if changed while connected.
  - single tracking session: second tab should be refused via status error / response error.
  - status broadcasts throttled to ~4/s. Use fire-and-forget, `.catch(() => {})`.
  - every handler try/catch updates `lastError` + broadcast.
- Add pure helpers:
  - `extension/lib/engine/decode-gate.ts` for accepting parsed frames and updating last decode; can wrap SequenceTracker or be a small helper.
  - `extension/lib/engine/channel-mapping.ts` with `resolveOutputs(state, map, devices, mode)` or equivalent pure function. Include types to support simple mode channel 0 -> all vibrators and mapped mode by deviceName/actuatorIndex with scale.
- Add Chrome offscreen entrypoint under `extension/entrypoints/offscreen/` that instantiates Engine and registers runtime message handling. Use WXT entrypoint include/exclude option to make it Chrome-only if needed; if HTML entrypoint cannot express that cleanly, keep it buildable and have background only create it on Chrome.
- Rewrite `extension/entrypoints/background.ts`:
  - Chrome: owns `engine/ensure` only, creating offscreen document via `chrome.offscreen.createDocument({ url: '/offscreen.html', reasons: ['WORKERS'], justification: 'Runs WebAssembly QR decoding and maintains the local Intiface device WebSocket' })`, guarded by getContexts check + in-flight dedupe.
  - Firefox (`import.meta.env.FIREFOX` or BROWSER check): instantiate Engine inline and route EngineRequest messages directly.
  - Message ownership rule: return undefined/false for types not owned; owners sendResponse + return true. Do not rely on promise returns from listeners.
- The Engine should expose enough methods for background/offscreen to register message handlers cleanly.
- Keep C1 validators in use (`isEngineRequest`, `isEnsureEngineRequest`).

Verification:
- `npm run compile -w qrtuber-extension`
- `npm run build -w qrtuber-extension`
- `npm run build:firefox -w qrtuber-extension`
- Inspect generated manifests/outputs enough to confirm offscreen.html exists for Chrome and Firefox still builds.

Final response: list changed files and verification results. If you must make a small C1 type correction to compile, note it explicitly and keep it minimal."}

{"branch":"master","confidence":90,"prompt":"Repository: /Users/qdot/code/qrtuber

Implement Stage D1 from plans/fill-in-the-rest-implementation.md. Do not commit.

Ownership/write scope:

Do not edit extension/docs/core or package metadata. You are not alone in the codebase; do not revert unrelated edits.

Requirements:
- Build the WebRTC viewer capture/decode loop using React 19 and existing coreBridge imports.
- Add `src/shared/browser.ts`: `isFirefox`; getDisplayMedia constraints with Chrome-only hints `selfBrowserSurface:'exclude'`, `surfaceSwitching:'include'` behind a local type extension.
- Add `src/viewer/useDisplayCapture.ts`: start/stop display capture, handle track-ended cleanup.
- Add `src/viewer/tickWorker.ts`: dedicated Web Worker setInterval ticks the decode loop. Main thread/rAF timers can throttle in background, so this worker tick is required.
- Add `src/viewer/useDecodeLoop.ts`: owns `ContentVideoHandler` with explicit video element + `QRCodeFinder`; dedupes parsed frames with `SequenceTracker`; exposes lastFrame, hapticsState, boundingBox, stats, decodeError; computes full-frame overlay coords using `currentRegion` + crop-relative decode box.
- Add `CapturePreview.tsx`: video preview + scaled bounding-box overlay div.
- Add `StatusBar.tsx`.
- Replace `ViewerApp` placeholder in `main.tsx` with usable viewer UI, including Firefox notice: detach stream tab to a window, capture the window.
- Keep UI utilitarian and stable; no landing page; no nested cards. Use existing shared styles and add viewer styles as needed.

Verification:
- `npm run compile -w qrtuber-webrtc-viewer`
- `npm run build -w qrtuber-webrtc-viewer`
- If practical, static smoke that generator route still builds and viewer route imports without direct `qrtuber` except coreBridge.

Manual browser capture verification can be left for orchestrator/user; just report it was not run if not possible. Final response: list changed files and verification results."}

nodes.metadata_json:
{"branch":"master","confidence":90,"prompt":"You are implementing Stage C3 of plans/fill-in-the-rest-implementation.md in /Users/qdot/code/qrtuber. You are not alone in this codebase: other agents may make unrelated changes, so do not revert or overwrite work outside your scope. Do not commit.

Scope you own:
- extension/entrypoints/content.ts
- new extension/lib/content/** files only if needed
- small, necessary extension utility adjustments only if C3 cannot work without them; call them out explicitly.

Task:
Rewrite the extension content script so it owns ContentCommand messages only and validates them with the C1 guards. Implement TrackingController for the content capture loop using the existing core ContentVideoHandler because C-preflight recorded page-video pixel export as qdot-assisted viable for target platforms. Flow:
- content.ts should defineContentScript, register runtime messages, return undefined for messages it does not own, return true for async owned messages, and stop tracking on ctx.onInvalidated with reason 'navigation'.
- Content commands: content/ping, content/start, content/stop.
- On start: locate the largest visible <video>, notify engine with tracking/started, begin scheduled capture/decode loop.
- TrackingController should capture via ContentVideoHandler videoblob event, convert object URL blob to data URL (PNG/ROI path is acceptable via current handler blob; if you add JPEG search mode, explain it), send frame/decode to engine via sendToEngine, then feed the response back into ContentVideoHandler.handleQRCodeFinderReturn with a VisualDecodeResult-shaped object so ROI tracking works.
- Scheduler should use natural backpressure, no unbounded recursion: about 100ms while tracking, 500ms while searching/no result. One AbortController for page listeners. Five consecutive send failures should stop and report no-video; each send failure should re-ensure engine once via sendToEngine's behaviour and surface cleanly.
- Data crossing extension runtime must be JSON-safe data URLs, not blob URLs.
- Navigation/stop should cleanly tear down timers/listeners, stop haptics via tracking/stopped, and not leave pending loops throwing.

Verification to run:
- npm run compile -w qrtuber-extension
- npm run build -w qrtuber-extension
- npm run build:firefox -w qrtuber-extension
- rg "blob_url|intiface_command|QRTuberIntifaceClient|QRCodeFinderResult" extension/entrypoints extension/lib extension/utils (blob_url should not remain in extension content/background paths)

Final response: summarize changed files, verification, and any caveats. Do not commit."}

nodes.metadata_json:
{"branch":"master","confidence":90,"prompt":"You are implementing Stage C4 of plans/fill-in-the-rest-implementation.md in /Users/qdot/code/qrtuber. You are not alone in this codebase: another worker may implement C5 options at the same time, so do not revert or overwrite unrelated changes. Do not commit.

Own only:
- extension/entrypoints/popup/**
- new extension entrypoint-local popup hook/component files under extension/entrypoints/popup/** if useful
- Do not touch extension/entrypoints/options/**, extension/utils/**, engine/content/core/webrtc/docs unless absolutely necessary; call out any necessity.

Task:
Replace the vanilla popup shell with a compact React popup per Stage C4:
- React root in popup, kill all starter CSS/logo demo UI.
- Components can be inline or split: ErrorBanner, ConnectionCard, TrackingCard, DecodeIndicator.
- Hook/use logic: ensure engine on mount, status/get, subscribe to status/update broadcasts using isStatusBroadcast.
- Active-tab logic: query active tab, ping content with {type:'content/ping'}; if no content script, show an Enable on this page button that uses activeTab + browser.scripting.executeScript to inject the current content script into the active tab. WXT content entrypoint path may require browser.scripting.executeScript({target:{tabId}, files:['content-scripts/content.js']}); verify against build output if needed.
- Controls: Connect/Disconnect via sendToEngine intiface/connect|disconnect; Start/Stop via browser.tabs.sendMessage content/start|content/stop; master haptics toggle via haptics/set-enabled; gear opens options page; dismissible lastError banner.
- Display: status dot, device count, address readout, tracking state including no-video, session/seq, decode age green <1s amber <3s red stale. Popup width ~320px, compact, utilitarian.
- Use the C1 message/status helpers; no legacy content_command or intiface_command messages.

Verification to run:
- npm run compile -w qrtuber-extension
- npm run build -w qrtuber-extension

nodes.metadata_json: {"branch":"update","confidence":90,"prompt":"Now that we have a fully working encoder/decoder library and WebRTC application in this repo (see deciduous tree for mroe info), we need one more piece: A way to generate QRCodes that is interesting for streams. To start, we want to create another app mode in our WebRTC app, alongside viewer and generator, that will allow us to connect to Intiface Central as a Websocket Device. Stale information on this is available at https://buttplug.io/docs/dev-guide, though you can also just look at the code in ../buttplug, specifically the device_websocket_hwmgr crate. We should define which protocol we want to expect (probably either tcode or lovense, tcode is the most extensible, lovense defaults to being a single vibrator which will be useful for us), and for now we will have to live with whatever the default device implementation for the library will be because we have no way to set up websocket devices to map to a specific configuration in Intiface/Buttplug yet.\n\nIdeally, we want to arrive at a plan for this before implementing, to make sure all of the proper research has been done first."}

The app mode is a Lovense-compatible Intiface Central Websocket Device that generates QT1 QR codes from Intiface commands. Central setup should say: enable Device Websocket Server, add a Websocket Device with protocol `lovense` and device address/name `qrtuber-lovense`, start the engine, then connect the web app to `ws://127.0.0.1:54817`.
